action.reasoning: Observation: best 0.594 (iter16: replay=500/task balanced post-task via meta+torch.where/randperm AFTER train, KD beta=0.5/T=2.0 with KLDiv(log_softmax(new/T)||softmax(old/T))*T**2, consol=5@1e-4 on full mem, Adam(5e-4/1e-5), per-task Cosine reset, dense logs every20steps+val/best per epoch, deepcopy old_model). Recent iters17-26 all failed (~0.08-0.58, last 0.0828) due to persistent train.py truncation (ends mid-train loop at 'labels.to(d'; missing opt.step/scheduler/KD/epochs/mem_update_after_task/consol/best-tracking; late tasks random acc~0.04 loss~3+, val~0.07). Curves confirm no post-task0 learning. Analysis: long train.py triggers workspace truncation in context; successful iter16 had COMPLETE code with all components (val~0.596 matching test, balanced task accs early~0.48/late~0.7-0.8). Root cause fixed by concise full rewrite. Decision: overwrite with COMPLETE concise non-truncated train.py exactly replicating iter16 success params (update mem AFTER task train only on current data, full KD only if task>0, per-epoch val+best on val.pt, final best ckpt with model_state_dict). Update predict.py for robust loading (handles state_dict or full ckpt with 'model_state_dict', correct test transforms/inference). Run full train then submit_eval on checkpoint.pt to recover >=0.594 (target >0.60).
